tax: Placed at genus rank, Bladeling is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a fibrous gypsum body that cleaves lengthwise. A new species belongs here only if it shows a cleaving gypsum body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by an upright fanning blade-sheaf. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — silky fibre-heaps — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Bladeling occupies gypsum-curtain chambers. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a sulphate seep grows fibrous selenite and recur with the evaporite seep refeeds the fibres, sustaining themselves on sulphate supersaturation.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: sweeps self-cleaving blades to touch. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: fibrous body and satin sheen. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: self-sharpening cleaving blades. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Bladeling matters because its soft sheen hides a live edge. It lets survey reports sort soft cleaving forms from hard ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Bladeling is the genus-rank grouping of fibrous-gypsum crystalline elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a fibrous selenite body whose blades cleave and re-sharpen, and divide below into species groups by fibre length and seep sulphate strength. Silent and endlessly self-sharpening.
